title: Carnival sees record January booking week for Holland America Line
text: Carnival Corp. CCL, +3.67% CCL, -1.45% said Friday that its premium cruise line Holland America Line saw record bookings for the third week of January in a good early sign of demand during the “Wave season.” The cruise operator said bookings for the week ended Jan. 20 were 20% above bookings seen in the same week of pre-pandemic 2019, and that 25% of the bookings were for cruises in 2024.

title: Hotel REITs: Dividends Are Back
text: Despite lingering recession concerns and recent travel disruptions, Hotel REITs have been among the better-performing sectors over the past year, buoyed by steady post-pandemic operating improvement fueling long-awaited dividend resumptions. Several years of pent-up leisure demand helped to offset a sluggish business and group travel recovery. Hotel revenues eclipsed record highs in 2022, but with wide dispersion between markets and segments.

title: Cruise industry poised to make strong comeback in 2023
text: More than 50% of U.S. adults say that they are just as likely or more likely to consider taking a cruise this year as they were during pre-pandemic days, according to AAA.

title: Carnival Surges on Management's Bullish Outlook
text: Cruise stocks such as Carnival Corp. (CORP) surged on Wednesday after Carnival announced its intention to raise on-board and add-on prices. Much ado has been made of the announcement as it signals pricing confidence from a company that has been under pressure during the past few years amid the Covid-19 pandemic.
